subject: Project Management
title: Lean in the Military
description: A quick win: understand Lean in the Military.

hook.text: The U.S. Army adopted lean principles to maintain equipment faster in combat zones.

buildup.text: Repair depots used lean to reduce helicopter maintenance turnaround from 238 days to 100.

discovery.text: Value stream mapping showed that helicopters spent most of their repair time waiting — not being fixed.

twist.text: The military's rigid hierarchy clashed with lean's emphasis on empowering frontline workers.

climax.text: When lean was adapted to military culture instead of imposed, results improved dramatically.

punchline.text: Even armies need to learn from Toyota.

quiz.question: How did lean help the U.S. Army?
options: Reduced helicopter maintenance turnaround from 238 days to 100
